currentUse: Active Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran congregation worshiping as "St. John-Tėviškės Church" in the building of St. John Lutheran Church, 7214 S. Cass Ave., Darien, IL; Lithuanian-language services with Communion every Sunday 11:30 AM and Thursday 7 PM
summary: Tėviškės parapija is alive and worshiping in Darien, IL. The official website of the Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran Church (liuteronai.lt), current as of July 2026, lists the parish as active at "St. John-Tėviškės Church," 7214 S. Cass Ave., Darien, with Pastor Liudas Miliauskas and Lithuanian services with Holy Communion every Sunday at 11:30 AM and every Thursday at 7 PM; the parish's own site (teviske.org) gives the same schedule and contact (teviskes.parapija@gmail.com, 708-567-9044). A Draugas article of January 4, 2020 documented the move into St. John Lutheran Church in Darien after the Western Springs period, reporting overflow attendance at the first services and a congregation that had grown from near-decline in 2005 under Miliauskas, who by then had served 14 years. The host building belongs to St. John Lutheran Church, itself an active congregation (Sunday 9 AM services, own pastor, events scheduled into August 2026), so the Lithuanian parish now shares rather than owns its home. The parish's original self-owned building — a former synagogue at 6641-45 S. Troy St. in Marquette Park, consecrated as Tėviškės on December 15, 1957 — was sold to a Black religious congregation after the neighborhood's demographics changed, per Draugas's 2015 Lithuanian cultural heritage guide; the address today houses From the Heart Church Ministries of Chicago. As of July 15, 2026, a 9,935 sq ft church building at 6645 S. Troy St. (built 1925, adjacent to Marquette Park) is actively listed for sale at $361,000, sold as-is and needing brickwork and boiler repairs — very likely the former Tėviškės building, though the listing does not name its Lithuanian history. As a congregationally governed Lutheran parish, it faces no Catholic diocesan restructuring exposure.

developments.headline: Former Marquette Park home apparently on the market for $361,000
developments.detail: An active commercial listing (MLS 12619205, last updated 7/15/2026) offers a 9,935 sq ft church building at 6645 S. Troy St., Chicago — built 1925, adjacent to Marquette Park — for $361,000, sold as-is, 'needs fairly significant work - cosmetic, brickwork, boiler' but 'kept very operational by current ownership.' This matches the address block (6641-45 S. Troy) of the former synagogue Tėviškės bought and consecrated in 1957; the listing itself does not mention the Lithuanian history, so the match is probable rather than confirmed. A separate closed listing at 6641 S. Troy (same 9,935 sq ft footprint, MLS 6434461) shows a $475,000 sale price with no closing date visible.

developments.headline: Parish confirmed active in Darien with regular Lithuanian services
developments.detail: The Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran Church's official diaspora parish page, current in July 2026, lists Tėviškės parapija at St. John-Tėviškės Church, 7214 S. Cass Ave., Darien, IL 60561, Pastor Liudas Miliauskas, with Lithuanian services with Holy Communion every Sunday 11:30 AM and Thursday 7 PM. The parish's own website (teviske.org) and Facebook page carry the same schedule.

sources.title: Teviske - Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran Home Church

developments.headline: Move into St. John Lutheran Church in Darien; overflow attendance at first services
developments.detail: Draugas reported the parish's relocation from Western Springs into St. John Lutheran Church at 7214 S. Cass Ave., Darien — a building with real bells and pipe organs plus modern amenities. Pastor Liudas Miliauskas (serving since ~2005, 14 years at that point) said the parking lot could not hold all the vehicles at the first service; the congregation had grown from a declining state in 2005 into a vibrant body, with Thursday candlelit meditative services, Bible studies, and childcare during Sunday services.

sources.title: Kunigo džiaugsmas: nauja bažnyčia gausiai lankoma

developments.headline: Marquette Park building sold to a Black religious congregation (baseline from heritage guide)
developments.detail: Draugas's Lithuanian cultural heritage guide recorded that after Marquette Park's demographics changed, the Tėviškės prayer house at 6641-45 S. Troy St. (a former synagogue consecrated as Tėviškės on December 15, 1957) 'buvo parduoti juodaodžių religinei bendruomenei' (was sold to a Black religious community), and the parish moved to 5129 Wolf Rd., Western Springs. Today 6641 S. Troy is listed as the address of From the Heart Church Ministries of Chicago in Cook County tax-exempt records.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions; two refinements. (1) The archive's "voted to sell and relocate twice (Western Springs, then Darien Dec 2019)" is consistent with the sale of the Marquette Park building (Draugas 2015 heritage guide), but no source found confirms the parish OWNED and SOLD a Western Springs building — the 2015 guide and 2020 Draugas article describe the parish being AT St. James church / 5129 Wolf Rd., Western Springs, which may have been a shared or rented arrangement. (2) "other_self_owned" was true of the Marquette Park era (bought 1957), but the parish's current Darien home is the building of St. John Lutheran Church, an active host congregation — the parish now shares rather than owns, operating under the joint name "St. John-Tėviškės Church." The archive's "thriving and growing" characterization is supported by Draugas (Jan 2020) and by the parish remaining active with a twice-weekly service schedule on the LELB's official site in July 2026.
gaps: No sale price or exact year found for the parish's sale of the Marquette Park building (6641-45 S. Troy St.) — the 2015 Draugas guide confirms it was sold but gives neither; the $475,000 closed MLS listing at 6641 S. Troy is undated and cannot be tied to the parish's own sale. The identification of the 9,935 sq ft church currently listed at 6645 S. Troy ($361,000, active July 2026) as the former Tėviškės building is probable (matching address block, church use, adjacency to Marquette Park, 1925 construction consistent with a former synagogue) but the listing does not name its Lithuanian or Lutheran history, and the current seller is unnamed. The exact legal arrangement between Tėviškės and St. John Lutheran in Darien (merger, lease, or space-sharing) is undocumented publicly — St. John's own website makes no mention of the Lithuanian congregation. No 2024-2026 Draugas press coverage of the parish was found in open search (recent Draugas content is largely paywalled PDF archives). The parish's Facebook page could not be opened to verify week-by-week 2026 activity; the July 2026 liuteronai.lt listing is the recency anchor.
